description: Cross-language parity fixture for the MishiPass public ID format. The TypeScript Worker validation must accept all 'valid' entries and reject all 'invalid' entries.
alphabet_note: Crockford Base32. Excluded: I, L, O, U (visually ambiguous on printed tags).
canonicalization: Public IDs are canonical UPPERCASE only. Validation rejects any lowercase, whitespace-padded, or non-ASCII value. The country code is cosmetic display context, not validated against real countries, and contributes no uniqueness or security.
country_note: The CC segment is cosmetic display context only. It is NOT a security boundary. Uniqueness comes from the two random segments (S1, S2), not from CC.

invalid.reason: country code is lowercase — CC must be A-Z
I is excluded from the Crockford Base32 alphabet
O is excluded from the Crockford Base32 alphabet
country code contains a digit — CC must be A-Z only
segment characters are lowercase — format is case-sensitive
non-ASCII look-alike (Cyrillic А, U+0410) in country code
